title: bursera diversifolia
intro: Bursera diversifolia, also known as Copal or Palo de Aguila, is a species of tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Bursera diversifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as a natural antiseptic to treat wounds and skin conditions. Bursera diversifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional rituals and ceremonies in its native regions.
